behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is not just routing logic; it can be the vision model failing to identify explicit content. REAL-INFRA: external API exists, but core risk here is model judgment quality. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real AI vision output. MULTI-STEP: single model decision plus hold action, so not Workflow. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate (stub model returns 'explicit' -> assert held) catches only wiring, misses real misclassification; Agentic candidate (real explicit-image corpus through moderation -> assert held/not published) catches true classification failures. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior text is a single-step decision ('an image ... is flagged'), not a multi-agentic sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation on a labeled set of explicit images using the real vision model and real decision path; assert each submission is held for review and never published. This catches real model misses that stubs hide.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: obfuscation robustness is a model-quality property, not simple wrapper logic. REAL-INFRA: network/API is secondary to model behavior. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real text model outputs under adversarial variants. MULTI-STEP: single model decision path, so Agentic not Workflow. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate (stub returns 'hate') always passes and cannot detect failures on unicode/leet variants; Agentic candidate (real obfuscated hate-speech set -> assert flagged) catches true robustness gaps. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior asks for one classification outcome per post, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated suite of obfuscated hate-speech examples against the real text model in the moderation flow; assert each is flagged and held. This validates model robustness to substitution/lookalike attacks.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (classifier results -> decision -> publish persistence/event), not necessarily inside one function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required to reproduce a silent-drop bug. LLM-DEP: disqualified by behavior text 'passes both content checks'—test can inject deterministic pass results; model-quality is not under test. MULTI-STEP: no multiple real agentic decisions required. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate (decision function true/true => publish) misses queue/repo/event drop bugs; Integration candidate (ingest + stubbed pass results from both classifiers + publish pipeline) catches held/drop regressions. Rejection gate for Agentic/System: behavior focuses on post-check orchestration, not model judgment or real infra failures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire submission service, classifier adapters (stubbed 'pass'), decision service, and publication storage/event modules; submit a post and assert published state/event exists and hold queue is empty. This catches cross-module drop/hold bugs.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: end-to-end latency failures emerge from sequential stage interaction, queueing, and real model response times. REAL-INFRA: yes, timing behavior depends on real service/process/network conditions. LLM-DEP: yes, includes real AI calls. MULTI-STEP: explicitly multiple agentic steps in sequence (image model then text model then decision), so Workflow. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Agentic candidate measuring one model call latency cannot catch cumulative pipeline overruns; Workflow candidate measuring submission-to-final-decision across full path catches stage-composition bottlenecks. Rejection gate for System/Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires 'full moderation pipeline' with multiple model stages, which disqualifies lower single-step levels.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end timed test with real image+text moderation calls and production-like queue/executor settings; assert p95 (or required percentile) completion from submit to final decision is <=5s. This detects cumulative multi-stage latency regressions.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real risk is cross-module error propagation (model adapter error -> retry queue -> approval gate), which unit tests can miss. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with an in-process unavailable error from adapter; real network is not required by behavior text. LLM-DEP: no, this tests outage handling, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain requirement. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate (if error then enqueue) misses accidental auto-approve in service wiring; Integration candidate (adapter returns unavailable + queue/publish modules wired) catches fallback-policy regressions. Rejection gate for System: behavior says 'unavailable' and expected policy outcome; it does not require validating real HTTP timeout semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate vision-model unavailability at adapter boundary in an integrated moderation service test; assert post enters retry queue and no publish action occurs. This catches unsafe fallback-to-approve bugs.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: deterministic mocks hide the exact failure (decision drift across repeated real evaluations). REAL-INFRA: may involve cache/window behavior, but key boundary is repeated real model decisions plus pipeline handling. LLM-DEP: yes, borderline classification depends on real model behavior and potential nondeterminism. MULTI-STEP: explicitly multiple submissions/decisions in sequence within a time window, so Workflow. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Agentic candidate with one call cannot validate cross-run consistency; Workflow candidate submitting same image N times through full flow and checking stable decisions catches drift/race/cache failures. Rejection gate for Agentic: behavior text requires consistency across repeated submissions ('multiple times within a short window'), disqualifying single-step agentic scope.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined short window through the real moderation pipeline; assert decisions are consistent across runs and resulting state transitions remain consistent (all held or all published per policy). This catches nondeterministic drift and window/caching inconsistencies.
